name: plot_regulatory_score_distribution
description: Generate a histogram visualization of the Regulatory Score distribution from genomic data.

params.description: Generate a histogram visualization of the Regulatory Score distribution from genomic data.

rows.description: List of data rows, each containing regulatory_score and other genomic attributes

columns.description: List of column names in the dataset

image_base64.description: Base64-encoded PNG image of the regulatory score distribution histogram

statistics.description: Summary statistics including mean, stdev, min, max, and count of regulatory scores

execution.code:
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import io
import base64
from statistics import mean, stdev

def run(args):
    rows = args.get('rows', [])
    plot_type = args.get('plot_type', 'histogram')
    
    # Extract regulatory_score values from rows
    regulatory_scores = []
    for row in rows:
        try:
            score = float(row.get('regulatory_score', 0))
            regulatory_scores.append(score)
        except (ValueError, TypeError):
            continue
    
    if not regulatory_scores:
        return {
            'error': 'No valid regulatory scores found in data',
            'image_base64': None
        }
    
    # Create figure and plot
    fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(10, 6))
    
    if plot_type.lower() == 'histogram':
        ax.hist(regulatory_scores, bins=30, color='steelblue', edgecolor='black', alpha=0.7)
        ax.set_xlabel('Regulatory Score', fontsize=12)
        ax.set_ylabel('Frequency', fontsize=12)
        ax.set_title('Regulatory Score Distribution', fontsize=14, fontweight='bold')
    else:
        # Default to histogram if plot_type is not recognized
        ax.hist(regulatory_scores, bins=30, color='steelblue', edgecolor='black', alpha=0.7)
        ax.set_xlabel('Regulatory Score', fontsize=12)
        ax.set_ylabel('Frequency', fontsize=12)
        ax.set_title('Regulatory Score Distribution', fontsize=14, fontweight='bold')
    
    ax.grid(axis='y', alpha=0.3)
    
    # Calculate statistics
    mean_score = mean(regulatory_scores)
    stdev_score = stdev(regulatory_scores) if len(regulatory_scores) > 1 else 0
    min_score = min(regulatory_scores)
    max_score = max(regulatory_scores)
    
    # Add statistics text to plot
    stats_text = f'Mean: {mean_score:.4f}\nStd Dev: {stdev_score:.4f}\nMin: {min_score:.4f}\nMax: {max_score:.4f}'
    ax.text(0.98, 0.97, stats_text, transform=ax.transAxes, 
            fontsize=10, verticalalignment='top', horizontalalignment='right',
            bbox=dict(boxstyle='round', facecolor='wheat', alpha=0.5))
    
    plt.tight_layout()
    
    # Save to base64
    buffer = io.BytesIO()
    plt.savefig(buffer, format='png', dpi=100, bbox_inches='tight')
    buffer.seek(0)
    image_base64 = base64.b64encode(buffer.read()).decode('utf-8')
    plt.close(fig)
    
    return {
        'image_base64': image_base64,
        'statistics': {
            'mean': round(mean_score, 4),
            'stdev': round(stdev_score, 4),
            'min': round(min_score, 4),
            'max': round(max_score, 4),
            'count': len(regulatory_scores)
        }
    }
steps: Extract regulatory_score values from all rows
Create histogram with 30 bins showing frequency distribution
Calculate mean, standard deviation, min, and max values
Overlay statistics box on the plot
Render and encode as base64 PNG image
